title: Scripts for pseudo-time analysis of gene-polymerase cluster Interaction in oligopaint microscopy data
author: Hilbert, Lennart
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
MatLab image scripts for the pseudo-time analysis of the interaction of oligopaint-labeled genes with RNA polymerase II clusters. 

  Requirements  

 A MatLab installation with the Image Processing Toolbox is required, the parallel processing toolbox can increase performance. Additionally, the bfmatlab toolbox from the Open Microscopy Environment must be installed and added to the MatLab path. All data were processed on a MacBook Pro with 16 GB RAM, also 8GB RAM should bee sufficient, disk space of several 100 GB will be required for typical datasets. 

  Analysis Procedure  

 1) Download the required raw data in the Nikon .nd2 format. You need all data from the Sphere stage, for eight different genes. Store them in the folder structure as indicated in the top part of the script  MultiPosition_extraction_nd2.m  

 2) The raw image data must be extracted from the Nikon .nd2 format into MatLab data structures in the .mat format. This is done by the script  MultiPosition_extraction_nd2.m  In the top part of this script, you can enter all files that should be included in the extraction procedure. The script as provided here will extract only data from the Sphere stage, which is all that is needed for the pseudo-time analysis at this stage. You might have to specify the main folder where the raw data are stored, so that the script can locate them for extraction. 

 3) For a first "actual analysis" procedure, use the  OligoPaint_FeatureExtraction_klf2b.m  script. Make sure the directory path in the top of the script points to the location where your extracted data (.mat files) are saved, so the script can find the extracted data it needs. 

 4) To analyze all data from the Sphere stage and get a graphical overview of the results, use the  OligoPaint_FeatureExtraction_SphereStage_AllGenes.m  script. Again, it might be necessary to adjust the path in the top of the script so that the raw data are located correctly.

MatLab image scripts for the pseudo-time analysis of the interaction of oligopaint-labeled genes with RNA polymerase II clusters.

Additionally, the bfmatlab toolbox from the Open Microscopy Environment must be installed and added to the MatLab path. All data were processed on a MacBook Pro with 16 GB RAM, also 8GB RAM should bee sufficient, disk space of several 100 GB will be required for typical datasets.

 1) Download the required raw data in the Nikon .nd2 format. You need all data from the Sphere stage, for eight different genes. Store them in the folder structure as indicated in the top part of the script  MultiPosition_extraction_nd2.m  

 2) The raw image data must be extracted from the Nikon .nd2 format into MatLab data structures in the .mat format. This is done by the script  MultiPosition_extraction_nd2.m  In the top part of this script, you can enter all files that should be included in the extraction procedure. The script as provided here will extract only data from the Sphere stage, which is all that is needed for the pseudo-time analysis at this stage. You might have to specify the main folder where the raw data are stored, so that the script can locate them for extraction.
